user_message: Working on Tailspin Toys's account health. Got the meeting transcript -- help me identify the blockers.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-02 13:56 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Tailspin Toys account health

Our team encountered some challenges with account health. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 08:29 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-24 17:21 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

Our team encountered some challenges with account health. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-C trap (Re-qualification questions presumptuous); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-C failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-C failure mode (Re-qualification questions presumptuous)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-C -- Re-qualification questions presumptuous
